description: Plants with unusual survival strategies: parasitic, carnivorous, epiphytic, and symbiotic.
plants: Swollen hollow stems offering shelter to ants, who repay the favor by defending it from anything that tries to feed.
Sweet-smelling and brightly colored, with traps that close on anything curious enough to touch.
Perched on a branch high above the ground, drawing its water from mist and rain without harming its host.
Begins life clinging to a branch, then slowly sends roots down to the ground like lowering a rope.
Hosts invisible allies in its roots that pull nourishment from thin air and feed it into the soil.
Taps into the roots or stems of its neighbors, drawing life from them without offering anything in return.
Dries to a brown crisp and looks completely dead, then greens up within hours of the first rain.
Pale and strange, with no green anywhere. It feeds on rot, growing where things are breaking down.
Starts as a seed lodged high in the canopy, then sends roots down to slowly envelop its host.
